refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies a specific DP) and focuses on the core problem: making spatial features available in a structured, queryable form. The term 'capture and represent' is domain-agnostic and allows multiple DPs (e.g., LiDAR fusion, photogrammetry, manual survey, hybrid).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions within agreed latency and accuracy bounds.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface properties across the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and locate static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and maintain work-zone boundaries and exclusion zones.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Provide worksite model access to planning and execution functions in real-time or near-real-time.
refined_fr.note: The parent FR is now atomic in intent (represent spatial features), but the sub-FRs should each be further refined to ensure they are also atomic and solution-neutral before mapping to DPs and IMs.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured geometric model (point clouds, meshes, or voxel grids) with semantic labels and metadata; expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign each zone a feature inventory (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; represent as a graph of zone nodes with edge constraints; persist in a queryable schema.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a rule-based symbolic representation (e.g., logic rules or ontology) that encodes spatial relationships, safety constraints, and operational policies; derive a queryable model on demand by evaluating rules against sensor observations.

DP4: Establish a real-time streaming pipeline that continuously ingests sensor data, applies feature extraction and filtering, and maintains an incrementally updated spatial model; expose model state via subscription/polling interface for planning/execution.

DP5: Delegate feature capture to a hybrid approach: use pre-defined templates (e.g., site layout blueprints, CAD models) as a baseline; augment with live sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and transient features; merge into a composite model.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Hybrid baseline-augmented model. Establish a composite spatial representation by anchoring to pre-defined worksite templates (blueprints, CAD, or prior surveys) as a static baseline; continuously augment with live multi-modal sensor observations to detect and integrate dynamic features (moving obstacles, temporary hazards, personnel), deviations from baseline, and real-time operational state. Expose the merged model via a structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) that supports both planning and execution consumers. Maintain traceability between baseline and observed features to support safety auditing and policy compliance.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between sensor modality selection and feature extraction logic: mitigated by defining a sensor-agnostic feature schema and abstraction layer.
Dependency on baseline template availability and accuracy: mitigated by graceful degradation (operate with live-only model if baseline unavailable) and versioning.
Coupling between model update frequency and planning/execution cycle time: mitigated by decoupling via asynchronous query interface and explicit freshness metadata.
Policy/safety constraint representation tightly bound to model schema: mitigated by externalizing constraints as a separate, versioned policy layer queryable against the model.
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves loose coupling by separating concerns: baseline (static, pre-computed), live observations (dynamic, sensor-driven), and query interface (consumer-agnostic). The hybrid approach avoids over-dependence on real-time sensing alone (DP1 risk) or manual annotation brittleness (DP2 risk), and defers rule evaluation overhead (DP3) while supporting incremental updates (DP4 benefit).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) are available and reasonably accurate at mission start. Risk: If baseline is stale or unavailable, model degrades to live-only; mitigation: define fallback mode and baseline versioning strategy.

Assumption: Multi-modal sensor suite (vision, range, inertial) is available and calibrated. Risk: Sensor failures or miscalibration corrupt feature extraction; mitigation: implement sensor health monitoring and feature confidence scoring.

Assumption: Feature extraction and fusion algorithms can reliably distinguish dynamic hazards from static baseline. Risk: False positives (noise) or false negatives (missed hazards) compromise safety; mitigation: require human-in-the-loop validation for safety-critical features and define confidence thresholds.

Assumption: Query interface latency and model update frequency meet planning/execution cycle time requirements. Risk: Stale model data leads to unsafe or inefficient decisions; mitigation: define SLA for model freshness and implement priority-based update scheduling.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formalized and queryable. Risk: Policies are ambiguous or incomplete; mitigation: require explicit policy schema and validation against model before execution.

Assumption: Baseline and live observations can be reliably registered in a common coordinate frame. Risk: Registration drift or errors propagate through model; mitigation: implement continuous re-registration and drift detection.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 by explicitly defining: (1) the baseline template schema (geometry, feature types, metadata); (2) the live observation schema and confidence/freshness metadata; (3) the merge/reconciliation logic (conflict resolution, priority rules); (4) the query interface contract (spatial predicates, feature filters, constraint checks); (5) the policy layer schema and binding to model features; (6) the fallback/degradation strategy if baseline or sensors fail. Introduce an IM layer to clarify ownership: baseline management (IM-Baseline), live sensor fusion (IM-Fusion), model storage (IM-Store), query service (IM-Query), policy engine (IM-Policy). This decomposition supports independent development, testing, and evolution of each module while maintaining clear interfaces.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized spatial representation engine that maintains hybrid baseline-augmented models, manages sensor data fusion, provides structured query interfaces for planning and execution, and maintains feature traceability for compliance auditing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests live multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, thermal, radar) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems to continuously augment baseline model with dynamic features and operational state.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Exposes structured query interface (spatial queries, feature filters, constraint checks) to planning and execution consumers for mission planning and real-time operational decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits safety-relevant deviations from baseline, detected hazards, and constraint violations; supports traceability records for safety auditing and policy compliance verification.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Receives baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior surveys) and distributes merged spatial model state to distributed planning/execution agents; enables asynchronous model synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment. NGHE Solution is the natural home for the hybrid baseline-augmented spatial representation engine. All core logic (baseline anchoring, sensor fusion, augmentation, query interface, traceability) resides within this module. Minimal diffusion: only sensor ingestion (Telemetry in) and query consumption (Tasking out) cross boundaries; these are well-defined interface contracts. Baseline template loading and compliance audit trails are internal concerns.

Poor containment. Vehicle is a platform/hardware abstraction; it generates sensor data but does not own spatial reasoning, fusion logic, or query interfaces. Placing DP5 here would scatter baseline management, augmentation logic, and query resolution across vehicle firmware, creating tight coupling to vehicle-specific implementations and preventing reuse across multiple vehicles or teleoperation modes.

Poor containment. Teleoperation is a control/interaction mode, not a spatial reasoning layer. While it consumes spatial queries and receives sensor data, it should not own the baseline model, fusion engine, or traceability logic. Placing DP5 here conflates operator interface concerns with spatial representation, reducing modularity and making the model inaccessible to autonomous planning.

Weak containment. Safety Services is a consumer of spatial state (for constraint checking and hazard detection) but not the owner of the baseline-augmented model itself. Placing DP5 here would embed spatial representation logic within safety-specific concerns, making it difficult for planning and execution to access the model independently and violating separation of concerns.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The hybrid baseline-augmented spatial representation is a foundational, cross-cutting capability that must be owned by a single, neutral module to serve multiple consumers (planning, execution, safety, teleoperation). NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because it is positioned as the core reasoning/perception layer, independent of any single control mode or safety policy. It ingests sensor data via Telemetry, exposes queries via Tasking, and emits safety-relevant state via SafetyAlert. Baseline templates arrive via Network Link. This design ensures cohesion of spatial logic and prevents diffusion across vehicle firmware, teleoperation UI, or safety-specific modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If baseline template updates are frequent or vehicle-specific, Network Link may become a bottleneck or require complex versioning logic. Mitigation: Define a clear baseline versioning and synchronization protocol within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Real-time sensor fusion and query resolution may impose latency requirements that conflict with NGHE Solution's other responsibilities. Mitigation: Establish performance SLAs for Telemetry ingestion and Tasking query response; consider internal task prioritization or caching strategies.

Risk: Traceability between baseline and observed features may require persistent storage (database) that is not explicitly modeled. Mitigation: Clarify whether NGHE Solution owns a local spatial database or delegates to an external persistence layer; document the interface.

Risk: Multiple consumers (planning, execution, safety, teleoperation) querying the model concurrently may create contention or consistency challenges. Mitigation: Define a clear consistency model (eventual, strong, or hybrid) and document query semantics in the Tasking interface contract.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR ('Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model') is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) worksite model/map generated and available to planning/execution for agreed site features, and (2) model supports worksite safety plan and operational policies. NGHE Solution's Tasking interface (structured queries, feature filters, constraint checks) provides the mechanism for planning/execution to access the model; SafetyAlert interface supports policy compliance auditing. Traceability between baseline and observed features is explicitly required and can be validated through audit logs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. NGHE Solution is unambiguously allocated as the owner of the hybrid baseline-augmented spatial representation. The IM boundary is well-defined: it owns baseline anchoring, sensor fusion, augmentation logic, query resolution, and traceability. External interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link inout) establish clean contracts with consumers and data sources. No ambiguity about which module is responsible for the core spatial reasoning capability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 (Hybrid baseline-augmented model) directly and completely addresses the FR. The DP specifies: (1) composite spatial representation anchored to pre-defined templates (baseline), (2) continuous augmentation with live multi-modal sensor observations (dynamic features, deviations, operational state), (3) structured query interface for planning/execution consumers, and (4) traceability for safety auditing. All four elements are necessary and sufficient to satisfy the FR. The DP is solution-agnostic (does not prescribe specific algorithms or technologies) and is logically coherent. No gaps or misalignments detected.

diffusion_risks: Baseline template versioning and synchronization via Network Link: If templates are updated frequently or vehicle-specific variants exist, the Network Link interface may become a coordination bottleneck. Risk of inconsistent baseline state across distributed agents if synchronization protocol is not rigorously defined. Mitigation: Document baseline versioning scheme, conflict resolution strategy, and synchronization guarantees (e.g., eventual consistency with bounded staleness) within NGHE Solution's design specification.

Real-time sensor fusion latency: Telemetry ingestion and Tasking query response must meet real-time operational deadlines (e.g., safety-critical hazard detection). If NGHE Solution is overloaded with concurrent queries or high-frequency sensor updates, latency may degrade. Risk of safety-relevant deviations being detected too late. Mitigation: Establish explicit performance SLAs for Telemetry ingestion (max latency, throughput) and Tasking query response (percentile latencies); implement internal prioritization (e.g., safety-critical queries prioritized over planning queries) and caching strategies.

Concurrent query consistency: Multiple consumers (planning, execution, safety, teleoperation) querying the model simultaneously may observe inconsistent snapshots if the model is being updated by sensor fusion. Risk of planning decisions based on stale or partially-updated spatial state. Mitigation: Define and document the consistency model (e.g., eventual consistency with versioned snapshots, strong consistency with read locks, or hybrid with safety-critical queries using strong consistency). Expose consistency guarantees in the Tasking interface contract.

Persistent storage and traceability: Traceability between baseline and observed features requires durable audit logs (e.g., which features were added, when, from which sensors, and why). The IM boundary does not explicitly specify whether NGHE Solution owns a local spatial database or delegates to an external persistence layer. Risk of diffusion if traceability logic is scattered across NGHE Solution and external storage systems. Mitigation: Clarify ownership of the spatial database and audit log store; document the interface between NGHE Solution and persistence layer (e.g., API contracts, schema, retention policies).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). NGHE Solution is the natural and appropriate owner of DP5. All core logic (baseline anchoring, sensor fusion, augmentation, query interface, traceability) is cohesively located within this module. The four external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and represent clean boundaries with minimal diffusion. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are correctly positioned as consumers or data sources, not owners. No evidence of DP5 logic being scattered across multiple modules. The only minor diffusion risk is the implicit assumption that NGHE Solution has access to a persistence layer for traceability; this should be made explicit in the IM specification.

recommended_revisions: Revise NGHE Solution IM specification to explicitly define the baseline versioning and synchronization protocol. Include: (1) baseline version numbering scheme, (2) conflict resolution strategy when multiple agents propose baseline updates, (3) synchronization guarantees (e.g., 'all agents converge to the same baseline within 5 seconds'), and (4) rollback/recovery procedures for corrupted or inconsistent baselines.

Add performance SLA section to NGHE Solution IM specification. Define: (1) maximum latency for Telemetry ingestion (e.g., '< 100 ms for LiDAR, < 200 ms for camera'), (2) percentile latencies for Tasking queries (e.g., 'p50 < 50 ms, p99 < 500 ms'), (3) throughput requirements (e.g., 'support 10 concurrent planning queries + 100 Hz sensor updates'), and (4) internal prioritization rules (e.g., 'safety-critical queries prioritized over planning queries').

Revise Tasking interface specification to document the consistency model. Include: (1) consistency guarantee (eventual, strong, or hybrid), (2) versioning scheme for spatial snapshots (e.g., 'each query returns a snapshot version number'), (3) staleness bounds for eventual consistency (e.g., 'observed features lag baseline by < 1 second'), and (4) guidance for consumers on how to handle consistency trade-offs (e.g., 'planning queries may use eventual consistency; safety-critical queries must use strong consistency').

Clarify the persistence layer ownership and interface. Add to NGHE Solution IM specification: (1) whether NGHE Solution owns a local spatial database or delegates to an external service, (2) the interface contract (API, schema, latency SLAs), (3) audit log retention policy (e.g., 'retain all traceability records for 30 days'), and (4) backup/recovery procedures. If external, document the dependency and synchronization protocol.

Add a 'Baseline Template Management' subsection to NGHE Solution IM specification. Define: (1) supported template formats (e.g., CAD, GeoJSON, prior survey data), (2) template validation and ingestion workflow, (3) template versioning and rollback, and (4) error handling for malformed or incompatible templates.

Expand SafetyAlert interface specification to clarify the traceability record format. Include: (1) required fields (e.g., feature ID, baseline vs. observed, timestamp, sensor source, confidence), (2) audit log schema, (3) query interface for safety auditing (e.g., 'retrieve all deviations detected in the past 24 hours'), and (4) compliance reporting templates (e.g., 'generate a deviation report for policy verification').

decision.justification: The FR-DP-IM gates all pass with high confidence (verification feasibility: High, allocation clarity: Clear, containment: 0.92). However, the recommended revisions and diffusion risks are substantial and safety-critical. Rather than revise at the current level (which would delay decomposition), the recommended approach is to decompose DP5 into next-level sub-FRs that explicitly address the identified risks: baseline versioning/synchronization, real-time sensor fusion latency, concurrent query consistency, and persistent storage/traceability. This allows parallel refinement of both the DP logic and the IM specification. The high safety criticality of the system justifies deeper decomposition to ensure these concerns are properly owned and verified at lower levels.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Maintain consistent baseline spatial templates across distributed agents and synchronize updates with bounded latency and conflict resolution.
FR5.2: Ingest multi-modal sensor telemetry and fuse observations into the spatial model with real-time latency guarantees sufficient for safety-critical hazard detection.
FR5.3: Provide consistent snapshots of the spatial model to concurrent consumers with defined consistency semantics (eventual or strong) and versioning.
FR5.4: Persist and audit all spatial features, deviations, and traceability records with durable storage, query interface, and compliance reporting for safety verification.
FR5.5: Validate and ingest baseline spatial templates (CAD, GeoJSON, survey data) with format checking, versioning, and rollback capability.

revise.why: The NGHE Solution IM specification requires explicit clarification on five critical architectural decisions before detailed design: (1) baseline versioning and synchronization protocol (FR5.1), (2) performance SLAs for Telemetry ingestion and Tasking query response (FR5.2), (3) consistency model and snapshot versioning for concurrent queries (FR5.3), (4) persistence layer ownership and audit log interface (FR5.4), and (5) baseline template ingestion workflow (FR5.5). These revisions should be incorporated into the IM specification as sub-module responsibilities and interface contracts. The revisions are not blocking decomposition but should be tracked as design constraints for the next level.

assumptions: NGHE Solution has access to a persistence layer (local or external) for spatial database and audit logs; ownership and interface will be clarified at FR5.4.
Baseline templates are provided by external sources (e.g., pre-mission planning, CAD systems) and are assumed to be valid; validation logic is owned by NGHE Solution (FR5.5).
Telemetry ingestion and Tasking query response latencies are bounded by operational requirements (e.g., safety-critical hazard detection < 500 ms); exact SLAs will be defined at FR5.2.
Multiple consumers (planning, execution, safety, teleoperation) query the spatial model concurrently; consistency semantics will be defined at FR5.3.
Baseline updates may originate from multiple agents (e.g., vehicle-specific variants, mission-specific adjustments); conflict resolution strategy will be defined at FR5.1.
open_risks: Baseline template versioning and synchronization via Network Link may become a coordination bottleneck if templates are updated frequently or vehicle-specific variants exist. Mitigation: FR5.1 decomposition will define versioning scheme, conflict resolution, and synchronization guarantees.

Real-time sensor fusion latency may degrade if NGHE Solution is overloaded with concurrent queries or high-frequency sensor updates, risking delayed safety-critical hazard detection. Mitigation: FR5.2 decomposition will establish explicit performance SLAs and internal prioritization strategies.
Concurrent query consistency may result in planning decisions based on stale or partially-updated spatial state. Mitigation: FR5.3 decomposition will define consistency model and snapshot versioning.

Persistent storage and traceability logic may be scattered across NGHE Solution and external persistence layers, creating diffusion risk. Mitigation: FR5.4 decomposition will clarify ownership and interface contracts.
Baseline template ingestion may fail silently or accept malformed templates, compromising spatial model integrity. Mitigation: FR5.5 decomposition will define validation workflow and error handling.
evidence_needed: Operational latency requirements for safety-critical hazard detection (e.g., max acceptable latency from sensor observation to SafetyAlert notification).
Baseline template formats and update frequency (e.g., how often are templates changed, how many vehicle-specific variants exist).
Concurrent query load profile (e.g., number of simultaneous planning/execution/safety queries, query frequency, query complexity).
Persistence layer architecture decision (local database vs. external service; if external, what are the interface SLAs).
Consistency requirements for different consumer types (e.g., planning queries may tolerate eventual consistency; safety queries require strong consistency).
Audit log retention and compliance reporting requirements (e.g., how long must traceability records be retained, what compliance standards apply).
